diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic sign of upper GI bleeding source.
Confirms significant blood transit through the GI tract.
Major risk factor for peptic ulceration and bleeding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia, delayed capillary refill, confirmed melaena on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Upper GI bleed with haemorrhagic shock
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Coffee-ground vomiting, melaena, and postural presyncope in a patient on regular NSAIDs together confirm a major upper GI bleed requiring emergency transfer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate IV fluid resuscitation and urgent upper endoscopy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive diagnosis and haemostasis for the bleeding source.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and coagulation studies, group and hold/crossmatch for possible transfusion
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses degree of blood loss and prepares for transfusion if needed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Continuing NSAIDs with food and monitoring at the facility overnight
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would dangerously worsen the bleeding and delay life-saving treatment.
